threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when the bus screeches to a stop for someone just stepping off the curb. Locals barely even blink.
Caught the 59 last night, and half the passengers were singing along to a busker's guitar at Streatham Hill. Never a dull moment!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really here when I saw an old man using his rollator to navigate around the potholes as kids played ball in the street, yelling with joy.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a whole street of stalls selling nothing but homemade döner sauces in Neukölln. It's an odd little treasure that feels uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to keep an eye out for those late-night taco trucks. A true SF resident wouldn’t dream of hitting up a chain after 10 p.m.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy play the ukulele on the 38 Geary, while a woman serenaded him with opera. Just another Tuesday in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s plazas are for relaxing, but locals know they’re just as often sites for spontaneous concerts and protests. Quite the vibe!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still carry a map in their hands, oblivious that their smartphone is a better guide around these streets.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'tube' when everyone knows it's all about the Overground here. That's a rookie mistake!
Caught the N109 last night, and a bloke tried to haggle with the driver for a free ride—said he only needed to go a stop. Driver just laughed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the same guy in my kiez, fixing shoes under the bridge every Saturday, it hit me: this is home, full of character and quirks.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a bar where they serve craft beer from a vintage VW van parked inside. The vibe is as quirky as the locals.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the dodgy corners—knowing which taco truck's got the best al pastor on a Wednesday makes all the difference.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy feeding his dog a burrito on the 24-Divisadero. Muni is the only place where breakfast tacos and doggie brunch collide.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the tiny plazas where locals gather with their tascas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They're the ones still asking for extra pa amb tomàquet at every meal, thinking they’ve discovered a local secret.
